summary: Ukrainian drones have struck key logistics points including the Chonhar bridge and energy facilities in Luhansk, while Russian forces report advances on the Krasnoliman front. A threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Taganrog has been highlighted, and Russian drones continue attacks on Zaporizhzhia causing fires and casualties. International attention grew after Romania identified a Ukrainian drone that exploded in Constanta port.
key_points: Ukrainian drone strike damaged the Chonhar bridge leading to logistical challenges for Russian forces in Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/81747).
A warning was issued about possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Taganrog, Rostov region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67508).
Russian drones attacked Zaporizhzhia, causing a fire, infrastructure damage, three civilian deaths and one injured woman (Source: @UkraineNow/81748).
Ukrainian drones hit energy and transport infrastructure across Luhansk, including an electric substation, railway assets, a fuel depot and border points (Source: @eRadarrua/95811).
Russian forces are fighting for the village of Stary Karavan on the Krasnoliman front, clearing Ukrainian fighters and advancing along a road (Source: @tass_agency/379621).
Ukrainian Special Operations pilots used Hornet attack drones to destroy vehicle convoys on the Melitopol‑Chonhar corridor, disrupting Russian logistics (Source: @DeepStateUA/23553).
Romanian President said the drone that exploded in Constanta port was Ukrainian (Source: @tass_agency/379596).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian drones have struck the Chonhar bridge, disrupting Russian supply routes to Crimea, and hit energy and transport assets in Luhansk. Russian forces report combat for Stary Karavan on the Krasnoliman front while a threat of Iskander‑M missile launches from Taganrog is publicized. Drone activity continues on both sides, with Russian attacks on Zaporizhzhia causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage.

points: Ukrainian drones damaged the Chonhar bridge, a critical link to Crimea, forcing Russian logistics to seek alternative routes (Source: @UkraineNow/81747).
Special Operations pilots employed Hornet attack drones to destroy vehicle convoys on the Melitopol‑Chonhar corridor, hindering Russian fuel and ammunition supplies (Source: @DeepStateUA/23553).

In Luhansk, Ukrainian drones struck an electric substation near Rodakove, railway infrastructure, a fuel depot, and border checkpoint Izvarine, targeting Russian logistical lines (Source: @eRadarrua/95811).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone hit on a car carrying Oleg Tyagnibok, leader of the Svoboda party, in Ivano‑Frankivsk; he sustained injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30723).

Ukrainian intelligence noted the presence of multiple Shahed loitering munitions over the Black Sea near Chornomorsk and Odessa, indicating continued aerial pressure (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67513, @mon1tor_ua/67514).
Russian drones attacked Zaporizhzhia, igniting a fire at an infrastructure site, killing three civilians and injuring a woman (Source: @UkraineNow/81748).
Russian forces engaged in combat for the village of Stary Karavan on the Krasnoliman front, clearing Ukrainian fighters and advancing along the road (Source: @tass_agency/379621).
A Romanian official identified a Ukrainian drone that exploded in Constanta port, confirming cross‑border drone activity (Source: @tass_agency/379596).
Russian authorities reported the use of explosive devices hidden in water bottles, warning of dangerous handling (Source: @tass_agency/379618).
Russia announced a potential launch of Iskander‑M ballistic missiles from Taganrog, heightening regional threat levels (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67508).
Ukrainian drones were observed over Sumy heading south toward Romny, indicating reconnaissance or strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/64319).
A hostile reconnaissance UAV flew over Poltava moving southward, suggesting monitoring of Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/64306).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on a vehicle carrying Svoboda party leader near Ivano‑Frankivsk, causing injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30723).
Multiple UAVs were tracked moving from Sumy toward Chernihiv and Lviv regions, showing broad operational reach (Source: @kpszsu/64321, @kpszsu/64301).
Ukrainian drones targeted logistics routes in the Melitopol‑Chonhar corridor, disrupting Russian supply lines (Source: @DeepStateUA/23553).
UAV activity was reported over northern Sumy heading toward Shostka and Krolevets (Source: @kpszsu/64333).
A hostile UAV was observed over northern Volyn moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/64309).
UAVs were tracked over northern Kyiv heading toward Pripyat (Source: @kpszsu/64305).
A UAV was seen over northern Rivne moving west (Source: @kpszsu/64304).
UAVs over northern Poltava near Lokhvitsa heading west indicate surveillance of front‑line positions (Source: @kpszsu/64308).
Russian drones attacked infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia, causing a fire and civilian casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81748).
Ukrainian drones struck energy and transport assets in Luhansk, including a fuel depot and railway facilities (Source: @eRadarrua/95811).
Ukrainian drones hit Odessa, igniting a non‑residential building, injuring a 41‑year‑old man, and damaging vehicles and gas pipelines (Source: @UkraineNow/81745).
Ukrainian drones were reported near the Black Sea heading toward Chornomorsk, indicating pressure on southern maritime assets (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67513, @mon1tor_ua/67514).
A Ukrainian drone strike damaged the Chonhar bridge, affecting Russian supply routes to Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/81747).
Multiple Shahed loitering munitions were detected over the Black Sea near Chornomorsk and Odessa, highlighting ongoing aerial threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67513, @mon1tor_ua/67514).
Ukrainian Hornet attack drones were used to destroy Russian vehicle convoys on the Melitopol‑Chonhar route (Source: @DeepStateUA/23553).
Russian UAVs conducted strikes in Zaporizhzhia, causing fires and civilian deaths (Source: @UkraineNow/81748).
Ukrainian UAVs operated from Sumy, Poltava and Kyiv regions, conducting reconnaissance and strike missions across multiple fronts (Source: @kpszsu/64301, @kpszsu/64305, @kpszsu/64306).
A warning was issued about possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Taganrog, indicating a potential escalation in missile warfare (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67508).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones damaged the Chonhar bridge, forcing Russian logistics to seek alternative routes to Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/81747).
A threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Taganrog, Rostov region, was announced (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67508).
Russian drones attacked Zaporizhzhia, causing a fire, three civilian deaths and one injury (Source: @UkraineNow/81748).
Ukrainian drones struck energy and transport infrastructure in Luhansk, including an electric substation, railway assets and a fuel depot (Source: @eRadarrua/95811).
Russian forces are fighting for the village of Stary Karavan on the Krasnoliman front, advancing along a road (Source: @tass_agency/379621).
Ukrainian Special Operations used Hornet attack drones to destroy vehicle convoys on the Melitopol‑Chonhar corridor, disrupting Russian supply lines (Source: @DeepStateUA/23553).
Romanian President identified the exploded drone in Constanta port as Ukrainian (Source: @tass_agency/379596).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian drone operations are increasingly targeting Russian logistics, energy and transport nodes across multiple fronts, notably the Chonhar bridge and Luhansk infrastructure, degrading Russian sustainment capabilities. Russian aerial attacks continue to pose a civilian risk, as seen in Zaporizhzhia, while the announced Iskander‑M missile threat from Taganrog could raise the conflict's escalation potential. Both sides are employing UAVs for reconnaissance and strike roles, indicating a growing reliance on unmanned systems.
sections.regional_response: Romania publicly attributed a drone explosion in Constanta to Ukraine, reflecting heightened regional scrutiny of cross‑border drone activity. International observers note the risk of escalation with the Iskander‑M missile warning, potentially prompting NATO to reassess air defense postures in neighboring states. Ukrainian diplomatic outreach, such as Poland's request to rename a special‑operations unit, underscores ongoing political coordination among allies.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV traffic is dense across northern, eastern and southern sectors, suggesting coordinated Ukrainian surveillance and strike campaigns. Russian ground advances on the Krasnoliman front indicate a focus on securing supply routes toward Crimea, but Ukrainian interdiction of bridges and corridors may impede these efforts. The mix of loitering munitions, attack drones and potential ballistic missile use points to a diversified threat environment requiring multi‑layered air defense and rapid response capabilities.
